project.title: O-Glycosylation of Mycobacteriophage Proteins
project.projectDescription: Provided herein is the first evidence that mycobacteriophage, indeed phage of any kind, have surface proteins (capsid and/or tail tube proteins) that are O-glycosylated. The glycosyltranferases that glycosylate these proteins are encoded in the phage genomes. In addition, for the three mycobacteriophages invested in this study, the glycans appear to be attached exclusively at a C-terminal serine residue. Each phage expresses a unique and novel O-glycan. The LC-MS files deposited here are the preliminary evidence that these mycobacteriophage proteins are glycosylated. A follow-up investigation will examine the composition and structure of the O-glycans in more detail.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: The phages were expressed with M smegmatis, the host bacterium. The phage were isolated and the proteins were resolved by SDS-PAGE. Glycosylated protein bands were identified by staining with glycoprotein stain. Equivalent bands from a Coosmassie stained gel were excised and digested with trypsin. The resulting digests were initially screened by nanoLC-MS/MS using Q-TOF mass spectrometers. Subsequent LC-MS analysis was performed on an Eclipse Orbitrap mass spectrometer.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: The protein bands were identified using Mascot to match the peptide MS/MS spectra against a custom mycobacteriophage protein sequence database. Glycopeptides were identified by hand.
